zh.Dorchester, Massachusetts Bay, British C: 布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼多爾斯特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Clapp於1608年出生在英國戴文維恩 奧特利時，她的父親Nicholas Clapp33歲，母親Elizabeth Yonge29歲。她於大約1631年與John Pope在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼多爾斯特結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1655年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼多爾斯特享年47歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Clapp于1608年出生在英国戴文維恩 奧特利时，她的父亲Nicholas Clapp33岁，母亲Elizabeth Yonge29岁。她于大约1631年与John Pope在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼多爾斯特结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1655年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼多爾斯特享年47岁。

nameMeanings.name: Clapp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Devon and Somerset): either from Middle English clop(pe), clap(pe) ‘lump’, perhaps denoting a stocky person, or Middle English clap(pe) ‘loud noise; noisy, idle chatter’.
In some cases also an Americanized form of Slovenian Klep: nickname from klep ‘sharpness of scythe or sickle’, also ‘sharpening with a hammer’.

variants.name: Klapp
meanings: German: nickname for a gossip or slanderer, from Middle High German klapf, klaff ‘prattle, malicious gossip’. Compare Clapp .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Clapp was born in 1608, in Venn Ottery, Devon, England, her father, Nicholas Clapp, was 33 and her mother, Elizabeth Yonge, was 29. She married John Pope about 1631, in Dorchester, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1655, in Dorchester, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 47.

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Clapp(이)가 1608년 영국 데폰 벤 오터이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Nicholas Clapp(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Yonge(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 대략 1631년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 도르체스터에서 John Pope(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1655년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 도르체스터에서 47세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Clapp nasceu em 1608, em Venn Ottery, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, Nicholas Clapp, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Yonge, tinha 29 anos. Ela casou-se com John Pope aproximadamente 1631, em Dorchester, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1655, em Dorchester, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 47 anos.

ru.Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay C: дорчестир, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика

ru.Dorchester, Massachusetts Bay, British C: дорчестир, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Clapp родилась в 1608 г. в местности венн оттири, девон, Англия, её отцу по имени Nicholas Clapp было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Yonge, 29. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Pope около 1631 г. в местности дорчестир, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1655 г. в местности дорчестир, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 47 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Clapp nació en 1608, en Venn Ottery, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, Nicholas Clapp, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Yonge, tenía 29 años. Se casó con John Pope aproximadamente 1631, en Dorchester, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1655, en Dorchester, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 47 años.
